title: Jedi Apprentice: The Death of Hope
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:JA15Death_of_Hope.jpg]] |title=''Jedi Apprentice: The Death of Hope'' |author=[[Jude Watson]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |cover artist=[[Cliff Nielsen]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[October]] [[2001]] |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages= |isbn=0 439 13934 1 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[41 BBY/Legends|41 BBY]] |series=''[[Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice]]'' |preceded by=[[Jedi Apprentice: The Ties That Bind|''The Ties That Bind'']] |followed by=[[Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance|''The Call to Vengeance'']] }} '''''Jedi Apprentice: The Death of Hope''''' is the fifteenth in a series of young reader novels that chronicle the adventures of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] prior to [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. ==Publisher's summary==
'''Peace over Anger
Honor over Hate
Strength over Fear'''
''"I pledge myself to you, Tahl."'' ''"I pledge myself to you, Qui-Gon."'' ''With these words, two Jedi Knights acknowledged that their bond had grown beyond friendship and into love.'' ''Now Tahl has been abducted in a trap that Qui-Gon feels he should have foreseen. Consumed by dark visions, Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan Kenobi must do everything in their power to get her back before it's too late. But that won't be easy in a land where nobody can be trusted…and everyone has something to hide.'' ==Plot summary== Blinded from injuries sustained at [[Melida/Daan/Legends|Melida/Daan]], [[Tahl]] is nonetheless a great [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] renowned for her [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] skills. She shares a powerful friendship with [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]]; the two are connected through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] in a way that's stronger than friendship, deeper than love. Now, Qui-Gon experiences great loss as Tahl is kidnapped in a trap he should have foreseen. He and his young [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], set out to rescue her. They set out on the trail of [[Balog (Human)|Balog]], Tahl's kidnapper, with a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] tracking him they got from [[Mota]]. While pursuing Balog, they encounter [[Eritha]], who reveals to them her sister [[Alani]]'s plot to take over the government. She joins them on their mission. Soon after she joins them, they are attacked by the [[Rock Workers]], a group of [[Miner/Legends|miners]] whose lives are endangered by the [[Absolutes]]. When the Rock Workers attack, Obi-Wan is injured. They take him back to the [[Workers]]' settlement for [[Medcenter/Legends|medical treatment]]. While Obi-Wan is healing, Qui-Gon questions the workers about the Absolutes, hoping to get some leads on Balog and Tahl's whereabouts, but finds out nothing that could help them. As soon as Obi-Wan is fit to travel, they set off again, only to be delayed once more when [[Yanci]], the Rock Worker [[Doctor/Legends|medic]] who treated Obi-Wan, begs them to come back because the Absolutes are attacking their settlement. Qui-Gon sends Obi-Wan and Eritha to go help, but decides to pursue Balog himself. However, he follows the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]] and decides to go help the Rock Workers instead. By the time help got there, everybody had already been [[Death/Legends|killed]]. However, it did give them a lead on where the Absolutes were from the type of soil carried in from their [[Clothing/Legends|boots]]. The Jedi and Eritha were able to locate the Absolute hideout. They infiltrated it and found Balog with Tahl in a [[Sensory deprivation containment device|sensory deprivation device]]. Balog realized the Absolute hideout would no longer be hidden so he ordered an evacuation and destroyed it. They got Tahl out and swam in a [[Water/Legends|lake]] where the hideout was hidden away from it. Qui-Gon took Eritha's [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] and took Tahl to the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[New Apsolon/Legends|New Apsolon]] to be received by medics. While Qui-Gon is waiting for word from the doctor, Obi-Wan and Eritha arrive. They think about Tahl's kidnapping and if anyone else was behind it. Then the doctor comes and tells Qui-Gon that Tahl's damage is severe, but Qui-Gon is confident she will recover. He goes into the room where Tahl is, and she promises that wherever she is headed she will wait for him. Then she dies. Obi-Wan feels a [[Disturbance in the Force/Legends|disturbance in the Force]], and rushes into the room where Tahl lay. Qui-Gon's grief for her shocks him, and Qui-Gon tells his Padawan that there was no help for him now, only revenge. ==Continuity== The novel included several flashbacks to [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn’s]] youth, but deftly avoided any scenes with his [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]], who was not identified as [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] until [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Episode II]] ([[2002]]) ==Media== ===Cover gallery=== This is a gallery of the different cover variations of ''Jedi Apprentice: The Death of Hope''.
